articles.title: Mortgage Costs Jump After War Starts
articles.summary: A new report shows that getting a mortgage now costs much more than before a war began. For a typical home loan of £250,000 over 25 years, the yearly cost has gone up by £788. This is because banks have raised interest rates and removed some cheaper deals since the US and Israel started strikes on Iran in late February. The average two-year fixed rate is now 5.28%, up from 4.83% in early March. For a five-year deal, it's £651 more expensive than two weeks ago. Experts say borrowers should plan ahead as rates might change more due to global events. First-time buyers might find it harder to get affordable loans.

articles.title: People Changing Easter Trips from Dubai to Spain
articles.summary: Many British holidaymakers are changing their Easter holiday plans because of a war near Dubai. They are moving away from Dubai and other places in the Middle East to safer and more familiar destinations. Travel companies say people are booking trips to Spain, Portugal, Italy, and the Caribbean instead. Flights to these places are filling up quickly. Some airlines have stopped flying to Dubai and nearby areas until June due to safety concerns. Experts warn that it might be hard to find available flights as many people are rebooking their trips. People want to go to places that feel safe and easy to reach, like Spain and Portugal.

articles.title: Ukraine and UK Team Up to Fight Drones
articles.summary: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky is visiting UK Prime Minister Sir Keir Starmer to sign a new defence pact. This agreement helps Ukraine and the UK work together to make and supply drones, which are small flying machines used in wars. The UK will also give money to build an AI centre in Kyiv, Ukraine's capital. The leaders want to support Ukraine against Russia's invasion, which has been going on for four years. They believe drones are important for safety and security. Zelensky is traveling to other countries to get more help and keep Ukraine's fight in people's minds. The partnership aims to use Ukraine's experience with drones and the UK's factories to make better defences.

articles.title: A Leader's Tough Choices with a Friend
articles.summary: This article talks about a disagreement between two leaders: Prime Minister Keir Starmer of the UK and President Donald Trump of the US. President Trump has been saying mean things about Prime Minister Starmer on TV, like that the UK used to be a great friend but now Starmer can't make decisions without asking others. This is awkward for Starmer, but it might not be all bad. The article explains that there is a fight over what really happened in their private talks. For example, Trump said he asked for aircraft carriers, but British sources say that never happened. Instead, the US made three requests: to use UK airfields for attacks (rejected), for defense (accepted), and to help keep a sea route safe (still talking). Starmer wants to talk to his team and other countries before deciding, which shows he is careful. Despite the criticism, Starmer feels confident he made the right choices to protect people and work with allies.

articles.title: What is the Spring Equinox?
articles.summary: Spring starts on Friday with the vernal equinox. This is a special day when the sun is directly over the equator, making day and night almost equal in length. It happens because the Earth is tilted, but during the equinox, it's not leaning toward or away from the sun. This means both the northern and southern parts of Earth get the same sunlight. People all over the world celebrate this day. For example, in Japan, it's a holiday, and in Mexico, they watch shadows make a snake shape on a pyramid. After the equinox, days get longer in the north and shorter in the south until summer.

articles.title: President Trump Talks About Iran and Oil Prices
articles.summary: A reporter named Liz Landers talked to President Trump on the phone. He was in a meeting about Iran. He said the Iranian military is very weak now. He talked about a place called Kharg Island, which is important for Iran's oil. The U.S. attacked it, but some parts are still working. President Trump said he might attack it again if Iran does not cooperate. He also mentioned electric plants in Tehran, the capital of Iran. He said he could destroy them, but that would cause big problems and take years to fix. He wants to wait for now. The reporter asked about oil prices going up, but the article does not say what he answered.
